  "text": "The Kubernetes API deprecation policy is enforced through several layers of Go source files:

## 1. Core Deprecation Logic

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation/deprecation.go`** is the central file. It defines:
- `IsDeprecated(obj, currentMajor, currentMinor)`: checks if an object implements `APILifecycleDeprecated()` and whether the deprecation version is <= current server version
- `RemovedRelease(obj)`: extracts the removal version string (e.g. "1.25") from `APILifecycleRemoved()`
- `WarningMessage(obj)`: builds the human-readable deprecation warning string (e.g. "apps/v1beta1 Deployment is deprecated in v1.8+, unavailable in v1.16+; use apps/v1 Deployment")
- `MajorMinor(versionInfo)`: parses the current server version

## 2. Warning Injection at Request Handling

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go`** wires the deprecation system into route registration. For each API resource route, it calls `deprecation.IsDeprecated`, `deprecation.RemovedRelease`, and `deprecation.WarningMessage`, then passes the warning strings to `AddWarningsHandler` and the deprecated/removedRelease flags to `metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc`.

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning/warning.go`** provides `AddWarningsHandler`, which wraps route handlers to inject deprecation warning strings into the response context on every request.

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning/context.go`** provides `AddWarning(ctx, agent, text)`, the low-level function that records a warning to the context's `Recorder`.

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/warning.go`** provides `WithWarningRecorder`, an HTTP middleware filter that attaches a deduplicating recorder to each request context, writing RFC 2616 `Warning: 299` headers to the HTTP response.

## 3. Metrics Recording for Deprecated APIs

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics/metrics.go`** defines:
- `deprecatedRequestGauge` (`apiserver_requested_deprecated_apis`): a stable Prometheus GaugeVec tracking deprecated API usage, broken out by group/version/resource/subresource/removed_release
- `MonitorRequest(...)`: sets the gauge and adds audit annotations (`k8s.io/deprecated=true`, `k8s.io/removed-release=<X.Y>`) for deprecated requests
- `InstrumentRouteFunc` / `InstrumentHandlerFunc`: wraps handlers to call `MonitorRequest` after each request completes

## 4. Removal Version Validation / Minimum Supported API Version

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/deleted_kinds.go`** is responsible for removing expired API versions from the API server at startup:
- `ResourceExpirationEvaluator` interface and `resourceExpirationEvaluator` struct
- `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator(currentVersion)`: creates an evaluator from the current server version, reading env vars `KUBE_APISERVER_STRICT_REMOVED_API_HANDLING_IN_ALPHA` and `KUBE_APISERVER_SERVE_REMOVED_APIS_FOR_ONE_RELEASE` for override behavior
- `ShouldServeForVersion(majorRemoved, minorRemoved)`: returns false if the API removal version <= current version (with special handling for alpha releases)
- `RemoveDeletedKinds(groupName, versioner, storageMap)`: iterates all registered resources and removes those that `shouldServe` returns false for (i.e., expired by lifecycle)
- `shouldServe`: calls `APILifecycleRemoved()` and `APILifecycleIntroduced()` on the versioned object to determine if it should be served

**`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apis.go`** calls `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator` to get the evaluator and then passes it through `InstallAPIs`, which calls `RemoveDeletedKinds` on each API group's storage map before installation.

## 5. CRD-Specific Deprecation Enforcement

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go`** handles deprecation for Custom Resource Definitions. It checks the `Deprecated` and `DeprecationWarning` fields on each CRD version and injects warnings via `warning.AddWarning`. The `defaultDeprecationWarning` function generates a default warning message for deprecated CRD versions.

**`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/validation/validation.go`** validates CRD deprecation fields via `validateDeprecationWarning`, ensuring `DeprecationWarning` is only set when `Deprecated=true` and meets character constraints.

## 6. API Lifecycle Code Generator

**`staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/prerelease-lifecycle-gen/prerelease-lifecycle-generators/status.go`** and **`main.go`** implement the `prerelease-lifecycle-gen` tool. It processes `+k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:deprecated=X.Y` and `+k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:removed=X.Y` tags in `types.go` files to auto-generate the `APILifecycleDeprecated()`, `APILifecycleRemoved()`, `APILifecycleIntroduced()`, and `APILifecycleReplacement()` methods in `zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go` files.

## 7. Generated Lifecycle Implementations

The actual deprecation/removal version data is stored in generated files across all API packages:
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/networking/v1alpha1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/networking/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1alpha1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/events/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/node/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/coordination/v1alpha2/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/certificates/v1alpha1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/storagemigration/v1alpha1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/storage/v1alpha1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/policy/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/authentication/v1beta1/zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go`

These files implement the `APILifecycleDeprecated()` and `APILifecycleRemoved()` interfaces consumed by the enforcement code above."
}

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics/metrics.go` defines:

25: // AddWarningsHandler returns a handler that adds the provided warnings to all requests,
26: // then delegates to the provided handler.
27: func AddWarningsHandler(handler restful.RouteFunction, warnings []string) restful.RouteFunction {
28: 	if len(warnings) == 0 {
29: 		return handler
30: 	}
31: 
32: 	return func(req *restful.Request, res *restful.Response) {
33: 		ctx := req.Request.Context()
34: 		for _, msg := range warnings {
35: 			warning.AddWarning(ctx, "", msg)
36: 		}
37: 		handler(req, res)
38: 	}
39: }

17: package warning
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: )
22: 
23: // The key type is unexported to prevent collisions
24: type key int
25: 
26: const (
27: 	// warningRecorderKey is the context key for the warning recorder.
28: 	warningRecorderKey key = iota
29: )
30: 
31: // Recorder provides a method for recording warnings
32: type Recorder interface {
33: 	// AddWarning adds the specified warning to the response.
34: 	// agent must be valid UTF-8, and must not contain spaces, quotes, backslashes, or control characters.
35: 	// text must be valid UTF-8, and must not contain control characters.
36: 	AddWarning(agent, text string)
37: }
38: 
39: // WithWarningRecorder returns a new context that wraps the provided context and contains the provided Recorder implementation.
40: // The returned context can be passed to AddWarning().
41: func WithWarningRecorder(ctx context.Context, recorder Recorder) context.Context {
42: 	return context.WithValue(ctx, warningRecorderKey, recorder)
43: }
44: 
45: func warningRecorderFrom(ctx context.Context) (Recorder, bool) {
46: 	recorder, ok := ctx.Value(warningRecorderKey).(Recorder)
47: 	return recorder, ok
48: }
49: 
50: // AddWarning records a warning for the specified agent and text to the Recorder added to the provided context using WithWarningRecorder().
51: // If no Recorder exists in the provided context, this is a no-op.
52: // agent must be valid UTF-8, and must not contain spaces, quotes, backslashes, or control characters.
53: // text must be valid UTF-8, and must not contain control characters.
54: func AddWarning(ctx context.Context, agent string, text string) {
55: 	recorder, ok := warningRecorderFrom(ctx)
56: 	if !ok {
57: 		return
58: 	}
59: 	recorder.AddWarning(agent, text)
60: }

17: package filters
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"fmt"
21: 	"net/http"
22: 	"sync"
23: 	"unicode/utf8"
24: 
25: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/net"
26: 	utilruntime "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime"
27: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning"
28: )
29: 
30: // WithWarningRecorder attaches a deduplicating k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning#WarningRecorder to the request context.
31: func WithWarningRecorder(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {
32: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
33: 		recorder := &recorder{writer: w}
34: 		req = req.WithContext(warning.WithWarningRecorder(req.Context(), recorder))
35: 		handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
36: 	})
37: }
38: 
39: var (
40: 	truncateAtTotalRunes = 4 * 1024
41: 	truncateItemRunes    = 256
42: )
43: 
44: type recordedWarning struct {
45: 	agent string
46: 	text  string
47: }
48: 
49: type recorder struct {
50: 	// lock guards calls to AddWarning from multiple threads
51: 	lock sync.Mutex
52: 
53: 	// recorded tracks whether AddWarning was already called with a given text
54: 	recorded map[string]bool
55: 
56: 	// ordered tracks warnings added so they can be replayed and truncated if needed
57: 	ordered []recordedWarning
58: 
59: 	// written tracks how many runes of text have been added as warning headers
60: 	written int
61: 
62: 	// truncating tracks if we have already exceeded truncateAtTotalRunes and are now truncating warning messages as we add them
63: 	truncating bool
64: 
65: 	// writer is the response writer to add warning headers to
66: 	writer http.ResponseWriter
67: }
68: 
69: func (r *recorder) AddWarning(agent, text string) {
70: 	if len(text) == 0 {
71: 		return
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	r.lock.Lock()
75: 	defer r.lock.Unlock()
76: 
77: 	// if we've already exceeded our limit and are already truncating, return early
78: 	if r.written >= truncateAtTotalRunes && r.truncating {
79: 		return
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	// init if needed
83: 	if r.recorded == nil {
84: 		r.recorded = map[string]bool{}
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	// dedupe if already warned
88: 	if r.recorded[text] {
89: 		return
90: 	}
91: 	r.recorded[text] = true
92: 	r.ordered = append(r.ordered, recordedWarning{agent: agent, text: text})
93: 
94: 	// truncate on a rune boundary, if needed
95: 	textRuneLength := utf8.RuneCountInString(text)
96: 	if r.truncating && textRuneLength > truncateItemRunes {
97: 		text = string([]rune(text)[:truncateItemRunes])
98: 		textRuneLength = truncateItemRunes
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	// compute the header
102: 	header, err := net.NewWarningHeader(299, agent, text)
103: 	if err != nil {
104: 		return
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	// if this fits within our limit, or we're already truncating, write and return
108: 	if r.written+textRuneLength <= truncateAtTotalRunes || r.truncating {
109: 		r.written += textRuneLength
110: 		r.writer.Header().Add("Warning", header)
111: 		return
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	// otherwise, enable truncation, reset, and replay the existing items as truncated warnings
115: 	r.truncating = true
116: 	r.written = 0
117: 	r.writer.Header().Del("Warning")
118: 	utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("exceeded max warning header size, truncating"))
119: 	for _, w := range r.ordered {
120: 		agent := w.agent
121: 		text := w.text
122: 
123: 		textRuneLength := utf8.RuneCountInString(text)
124: 		if textRuneLength > truncateItemRunes {
125: 			text = string([]rune(text)[:truncateItemRunes])
126: 			textRuneLength = truncateItemRunes
127: 		}
128: 		if header, err := net.NewWarningHeader(299, agent, text); err == nil {
129: 			r.written += textRuneLength
130: 			r.writer.Header().Add("Warning", header)
131: 		}
132: 	}
133: }

  "text": "The Kubernetes API deprecation policy is enforced through several layers of Go source files:

## 1. Core Deprecation Logic

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation/deprecation.go` is the central file. It defines:
- `IsDeprecated(obj, currentMajor, currentMinor)`: checks if an object implements `APILifecycleDeprecated()` and whether the deprecation version is <= current server version
- `RemovedRelease(obj)`: extracts the removal version string (e.g. "1.25") from `APILifecycleRemoved()`
- `WarningMessage(obj)`: builds the human-readable deprecation warning string
- `MajorMinor(versionInfo)`: parses the current server version

## 2. Warning Injection at Request Handling

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go` wires the deprecation system into route registration. For each API resource route, it calls `deprecation.IsDeprecated`, `deprecation.RemovedRelease`, `deprecation.WarningMessage`, then passes warnings to `AddWarningsHandler` and the deprecated/removedRelease flags to `metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc`.

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning/warning.go` provides `AddWarningsHandler`, which wraps route handlers to inject deprecation warnings into the response context on every request.

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning/context.go` provides `AddWarning(ctx, agent, text)`, the low-level function that records a warning to the context Recorder.

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/warning.go` provides `WithWarningRecorder`, an HTTP middleware that attaches a deduplicating recorder to each request context and writes RFC 2616 `Warning: 299` headers to the HTTP response.

## 3. Metrics Recording for Deprecated APIs

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics/metrics.go` defines:
- `deprecatedRequestGauge` (`apiserver_requested_deprecated_apis`): a stable Prometheus GaugeVec tracking deprecated API usage by group/version/resource/subresource/removed_release
- `MonitorRequest`: sets the gauge and adds audit annotations (`k8s.io/deprecated=true`, `k8s.io/removed-release=<X.Y>`) for deprecated requests
- `InstrumentRouteFunc` / `InstrumentHandlerFunc`: wraps handlers to call `MonitorRequest` after each request

## 4. Removal Version Validation / Minimum Supported API Version

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/deleted_kinds.go` removes expired API versions from the API server at startup:
- `ResourceExpirationEvaluator` interface
- `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator(currentVersion)`: creates an evaluator from the current server version, reading env vars `KUBE_APISERVER_STRICT_REMOVED_API_HANDLING_IN_ALPHA` and `KUBE_APISERVER_SERVE_REMOVED_APIS_FOR_ONE_RELEASE`
- `ShouldServeForVersion(majorRemoved, minorRemoved)`: returns false when removal version <= current version (the minimum supported version check)
- `RemoveDeletedKinds(groupName, versioner, storageMap)`: removes resources whose `APILifecycleRemoved()` version has passed

`pkg/controlplane/apiserver/apis.go` calls `NewResourceExpirationEvaluator` and passes it through `InstallAPIs`, which calls `RemoveDeletedKinds` on each API group's storage map before installation.

## 5. CRD-Specific Deprecation Enforcement

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go` handles deprecation for Custom Resource Definitions. It checks the `Deprecated` and `DeprecationWarning` fields on each CRD version and injects warnings via `warning.AddWarning`.

`staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/validation/validation.go` validates CRD deprecation fields via `validateDeprecationWarning`, ensuring `DeprecationWarning` is only set when `Deprecated=true`.

## 6. API Lifecycle Code Generator

`staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/prerelease-lifecycle-gen/prerelease-lifecycle-generators/status.go` and `main.go` implement the `prerelease-lifecycle-gen` tool. It processes `+k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:deprecated=X.Y` and `+k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:removed=X.Y` tags to auto-generate `APILifecycleDeprecated()`, `APILifecycleRemoved()`, `APILifecycleIntroduced()`, and `APILifecycleReplacement()` methods in `zz_generated.prerelease-lifecycle.go` files across all API packages.

## 7. Generated Lifecycle Implementations

The actual deprecation/removal version data is stored in generated files:

These implement the `APILifecycleDeprecated()` and `APILifecycleRemoved()` interfaces consumed by the enforcement code above."
